lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Hmm? Oh, hello, surface-dweller!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: I'm writing the definitive history of Dorgesh-Kaan!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Actually, I wonder if you could help me...

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: I know the ruler of Lumbridge is called Horacio, but I don't know what his title is. Can you tell me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's a duke.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Duke Horacio. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's a general.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: General Horacio. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's a king.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: King Horacio. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's a lord.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Lord Horacio. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: You've talked to the goblins on the surface, haven't you? Do you know the name of the god that the surface goblins worship?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nobody knows.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Oh well. Thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's just called the Big High War God.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was Guthix.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was [player name].

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: [player name]? What a terrible, fear-inducing name!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: I'm writing about the great war between the gods that happened thousands of years ago. Do you know the reason for this war?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was for the gods' own entertainment.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Just as I'd thought. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zamorak attacked the other gods.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Really? Thank you for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The other gods attacked Zaros.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guthix wanted to preserve balance so he made the other gods fight.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: A strange idea of balance! Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: What's the name of the group of surface-people that tried to destroy Dorgesh-Kaan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: H.A.M.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Hmm, I wonder what that stands for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It stands for Humans Against Monsters.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: So we're monsters, are we? Thanks for telling me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: B.A.C.O.N.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Hmm, I wonder what that stands for. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: P.O.R.K.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Hmm, I wonder what that stands for? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: B.E.E.F.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Hmm, I wonder what that stands for. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: What species are you, if you don't mind my asking? I know there are many different races living on the surface, but I don't know which one you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm a human!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah, good, that's what I thought.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: A human, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: That's a relief! I thought you were some kind of horrible ogre!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: A human, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah, of course. I thought you looked a little tall for a dwarf.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: A human, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Oh! I thought you were an elf. Surface species are confusing!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: A human, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm an ogre. Raah!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Eek! An ogre! I had thought you were human!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: An ogre, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah, that's what I thought!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: An ogre, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah, of course. I thought you looked a little tall for a dwarf.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: An ogre, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: And I thought you were an elf! What a silly mistake!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: An ogre, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm a dwarf!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Oh? I thought dwarves were shorter. I thought you were human!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: A dwarf, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: That's a relief! I thought you were some kind of horrible ogre!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: A dwarf, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah, yes, of course, you said.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: A dwarf, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Oh! I thought you were an elf. Surface species are confusing!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: A dwarf, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm an elf!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Oh? I thought elves were more graceful. I thought you were human!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: An elf, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: That's a relief! I thought you were some kind of horrible ogre!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: An elf, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Oh? I thought elves were more graceful. I thought you were a dwarf!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm an elf!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah yes, that's what I thought.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: An elf, hmm? Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this building?

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: This is the Dorgeshuun Council chamber!

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Whenever a decision needs to be made, the seven members of the Dorgeshuun Council meet here. The last meeting was when they decided to open up the city to surface-dwellers.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: In between meetings, I work here writing up the minutes of the meetings and working on the definitive history of Dorgesh-Kaan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about the history of the city?

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Of course! I've gone through all of my predecessors' notes and compiled the definitive history of the Dorgeshuun! What would you like to hear about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about the founding of Dorgesh-Kaan?

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Of course! *Ahem*

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god whose name has been forgotten.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The gods became bored and decided to make the mortal races fight for their amusement. The goblins were sent to fight in many bloody battles against each other and other races.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The war lasted for thousands of years, and millions of goblins died. Eventually, the evil god sent the Dorgeshuun tribe to fight a battle that they could not win.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: But General Bloodfist of the Dorgeshuun refused to fight in this battle. Instead, he commanded the Dorgeshuun army to march down into a fissure in the ground.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: General Bloodfist stood at the mouth of the fissure and defied the might of the evil god.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The evil god smote him with a mighty blast that shook the earth, but that blast also closed the fissure, sealing the Dorgeshuun away from the reach of the gods.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Lieutenant Strongaxe, now the leader of the Dorgeshuun, led the tribe deeper into the caves. They found this cavern, and here they founded Dorgesh-Kaan, city of the Dorgeshuun.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The god Zamorak attacked the other gods, starting a great war. The goblins were sent to fight in many bloody battles against each other and other races.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The other gods decided to remove the great god Zaros from power, and started a great war. The goblins were sent to fight in many bloody battles against each other and other races.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: In order to preserve balance, the god Guthix made the other gods fight against one another. The goblins were sent to fight in many bloody battles against each other and other races.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god called Big High War God.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god called Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god called Guthix.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god called Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Before Dorgesh-Kaan, the Dorgeshuun lived on the surface with many other goblin tribes. All goblins were ruled over by an evil god called [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about the Dorgeshuun civil war?

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Certainly! *Ahem*

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: At first, Dorgesh-Kaan was ruled over by generals, just as the tribe had been when it was on the surface. Each general succeeded the last by defeating him in combat.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The generals were advised by a council of elders, but the Dorgeshuun were no longer an army, and many of the people felt that the Council should have authority.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The people voted to become a republic, but General Bonehelm refused to give up power, and so the Dorgeshuun Civil War began.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The republican faction was larger, but the military faction contained most of the trained warriors and controlled the magical equipment that the tribe had brought from the surface.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: General Bonehelm was driven from the city, but dug his own base out of the caves nearby.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: General Bonehelm's army attacked the city using archers mounted on giant frogs. There was a bloody battle, and General Bonehelm was driven back again into his base.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: But General Bonehelm's mining had weakened the stone around his base, and the retreating army's footsteps caused the roof to collapse, crushing his army.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: That was the last time one Dorgeshuun has raised arms against another. Since then, the city has been ruled peacefully by an elected council.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about recent years?

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ah, this is where you come in! *Ahem*

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: In the twenty-ninth century since the founding of the city, one of the miners broke through a wall into the cellar of a surface dwelling. The miners blocked up the tunnel and went back into their mines.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: However, a human named [player name] found [his/her/their] way through the caves and made contact with the miners.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ur-tag, head of the Council, signed a peace treaty with Duke Horacio of Lumbridge, ruler of the town above the Dorgeshuuns' mine.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: But not all surface-dwellers were friendly, and a faction called H.A.M. attempted to destroy the Dorgeshuun.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The Council sent Zanik to explore the surface to see if the H.A.M. group was plotting anything. With [player name]'s help, Zanik defeated a H.A.M. plot to flood Dorgesh-Kaan.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Despite reservations from many citizens, the Council eventually voted 4-3 to open Dorgesh-Kaan to surface-dwellers.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: But not all surface-dwellers were friendly, and a faction called B.A.C.O.N. attempted to destroy the Dorgeshuun.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The Council sent Zanik to explore the surface to see if the B.A.C.O.N. group was plotting anything. With [player name]'s help, Zanik defeated a B.A.C.O.N. plot to flood Dorgesh-Kaan.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Despite reservations from many citizens, the Council eventually voted 4-3 to open Dorgesh-Kaan to surface-dwellers.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: But not all surface-dwellers were friendly, and a faction called P.O.R.K. attempted to destroy the Dorgeshuun.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The Council sent Zanik to explore the surface to see if the P.O.R.K. group was plotting anything. With [player name]'s help, Zanik defeated a P.O.R.K. plot to flood Dorgesh-Kaan.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Despite reservations from many citizens, the Council eventually voted 4-3 to open Dorgesh-Kaan to surface-dwellers.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: But not all surface-dwellers were friendly, and a faction called B.E.E.F. attempted to destroy the Dorgeshuun.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: The Council sent Zanik to explore the surface to see if the B.E.E.F. group was plotting anything. With [player name]'s help, Zanik defeated a B.E.E.F. plot to flood Dorgesh-Kaan.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Despite reservations from many citizens, the Council eventually voted 4-3 to open Dorgesh-Kaan to surface-dwellers.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ur-tag, head of the Council, signed a peace treaty with General Horacio of Lumbridge, ruler of the town above the Dorgeshuuns' mine.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ur-tag, head of the Council, signed a peace treaty with King Horacio of Lumbridge, ruler of the town above the Dorgeshuuns' mine.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: Ur-tag, head of the Council, signed a peace treaty with Lord Horacio of Lumbridge, ruler of the town above the Dorgeshuuns' mine.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: However, an ogre named [player name] found [his/her/their] way through the caves and made contact with the miners.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: However, a dwarf named [player name] found [his/her/their] way through the caves and made contact with the miners.

lines.speaker: Goblin scribe
lines.text: However, an elf named [player name] found [his/her/their] way through the caves and made contact with the miners.
